name: Voice User Interface (VUI) Design
description: Comprehensive guide to designing voice user interfaces for voice assistants, smart speakers, and voice-enabled applications
definition: Voice User Interface (VUI) is a speech-based interaction system that allows users to interact with technology through voice commands and spoken language.

platforms.accessibility: Voice interfaces for users with disabilities (blind, motor impairments)

practices: Be concise (spoken word is slower than reading)
Provide help when user is stuck
Offer alternatives when request can't be fulfilled
Avoid unnecessary pleasantries after first interaction
Use progressive disclosure (don't list everything at once)

greeting.first_time: Welcome! I'm [Name]. I can help you [primary functions]. What would you like to do?
greeting.returning: Welcome back! What can I help with today?
best_practices: Don't repeat full intro every time

open_ended.use: When options are many or unknown
open_ended.example: What would you like to order?
open_ended.risk: User may not know what to say

closed_ended.example: Would you like pickup or delivery?

directed.example: You can say things like 'Order a pizza' or 'Check my order status'. What would you like?

confirmations.implicit: Used for low-stakes actions. Example: 'OK, setting your alarm for 7 AM.'
confirmations.explicit: Used for high-stakes actions. Example: 'I'll charge your card $50. Is that OK?'
confirmations.best_practice: Always confirm destructive or financial actions

no_match.response: Sorry, I didn't catch that. Could you repeat?
no_match.after_2nd_failure: I'm having trouble understanding. You can say [examples], or say 'help'.
no_match.after_3rd_failure: Let me connect you to a person who can help.

no_input.timeout: OK, let me know if you need anything.

out_of_scope.response: I can't do that yet, but I can help you [supported actions]. What would you like?
out_of_scope.avoid: Just saying 'I can't do that' (dead end)

considerations.tone: Professional, friendly, casual, formal (match brand and audience)
considerations.vocabulary: Match user's language level and domain
considerations.humor: Use sparingly, avoid sarcasm (tone doesn't translate)
considerations.empathy: Acknowledge user frustration or celebrate success

sample_size.description: Variety of user utterances to account for
sample_size.example: User wants to order pizza - 'I want a pizza', 'Order pizza', 'Get me a large pepperoni', 'Pizza delivery'

one_shot.description: User completes task in single utterance

multi_turn.example:
User: 'Book a flight'
Assistant: 'Where to?'
User: 'New York'
Assistant: 'What date?'
User: 'Next Friday'

disambiguation.example:
User: 'Play Hamilton'
Assistant: 'I found the musical soundtrack and the audiobook. Which would you like?'
disambiguation.best_practice: Limit choices to 2-3, provide clear distinctions

slot_filling.example: Booking flight - collect: destination, date, passengers, class
slot_filling.best_practice: Ask for required slots first, optional later

context_carryover.example:
User: 'Weather in Seattle'
Assistant: '65 and sunny'
User: 'How about tomorrow?'
Assistant (remembers Seattle): 'Tomorrow in Seattle will be 62 and cloudy'

for_blind_users.importance: Voice UI is primary interface for blind users
best_practices: Provide audio feedback for all actions
Avoid relying on screen for critical info

for_motor_impairments.importance: Voice eliminates need for physical interaction

accent_diversity.challenge: Voice recognition may struggle with accents

best_practices.design_for_voice_first: Voice should work standalone (visual is enhancement)
best_practices.sync_visual_audio: Visual should match what's being said
best_practices.brevity_visual: Don't show walls of text (people will ask to scroll)
best_practices.touch_optional: Allow touch but don't require it

patterns.list_navigation: Voice reads items, visual shows list, user can scroll or say number
patterns.visual_confirmation: Voice says action, visual shows result
patterns.complex_data: Voice summarizes, visual shows detail (e.g., weather forecast)

script_testing.process: Write out conversations, walk through all paths

wizard_of_oz.description: Human plays role of voice assistant
wizard_of_oz.process: Users speak to 'assistant', human responds (simulates AI)
wizard_of_oz.benefit: Test before building, understand user language

user_testing.description: Test with real users and voice recognition

voice_recognition.mitigation: Train models on diverse speech, use context to disambiguate

natural_language_understanding.nlu: Understand user intent from varied utterances
natural_language_understanding.example: 'Book flight to NYC on Friday' → Intent: BookFlight, Entities: {destination: NYC, date: Friday}

text_to_speech.tts: Convert text responses to spoken audio
text_to_speech.quality: Use neural TTS for natural-sounding voices
text_to_speech.ssml: Speech Synthesis Markup Language for control (pitch, rate, volume, pauses)

wake_words.description: Activation phrase (Alexa, Hey Google, Hey Siri)
wake_words.privacy: Local wake word detection, cloud for processing

always_listening.concern: Device always listening for wake word

best_practice: Don't require login for basic features
data_retention.legal: GDPR requires data deletion and export

consent.requirement: Explain voice data usage before collection

best_practices: Provide help when user is stuck
Handle errors gracefully (reprompt, offer examples, escalate)
Test with diverse accents and backgrounds
Provide clear visual feedback in multimodal
Respect user privacy (clear indicators, delete options)
Design for accessibility (voice is critical for blind users)
Avoid dead ends (always offer next step)
anti_patterns: Dead ends ('I can't do that' with no alternative)
Relying on screen for critical info (voice-first!)
No visual feedback in multimodal interfaces

metrics.task_success_rate: % of users who complete task successfully
metrics.average_turns: Number of back-and-forth exchanges (fewer is better for simple tasks)
metrics.wake_word_accuracy: % of wake word detections that are true positives

amazon_alexa.strength: Largest skill library, smart home integration

google_assistant.strength: Best voice recognition, Google services integration

dominos_pizza.approach: Voice ordering via Alexa and Google
dominos_pizza.feature: 'Easy Order' - one phrase to order favorite

mayo_clinic.approach: Symptom checker and health info via voice

resources: Designing Voice User Interfaces (Cathy Pearl) - VUI design bible
Voice User Interface Design (Michael McTear et al.) - Academic perspective
Alexa Design Guide - Amazon's official VUI guidelines
Google Assistant Conversation Design - Google's guidelines
